body:
Title: Revenu Quebec Schedule T 2025 tax credit for tuition or examination fees.

Source:
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/en/online-services/forms-and-publications/current-details/tp-1-v/
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/documents/en/formulaires/tp/2025-12/TP-1.D.T-V%282025-12%29.pdf

Schedule T numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 20
Value: 0.20
Value: 8
Value: 0.08
Value: 5
Value: 12.5
Value: 100

Schedule T says only the student encloses the schedule. Part A calculates the tax credit for tuition or examination fees the student can claim and the fees that can be carried forward. It also states that the amount on TP-1 line 398 must equal the total of lines 38 and 46.

Part A section 1 covers tuition or examination fees that qualify for the 20% tax credit rate. Line 34 is the unused portion of tuition or examination fees from line 40 of Schedule T for 2024 or the notice of assessment. Line 37 multiplies line 34 by 20%. Line 38 is all or part of line 37 claimed on TP-1 line 398. Line 39 subtracts line 38 from line 37. Line 40 multiplies line 39 by 5 to determine the amount that can be carried forward.

Part A section 2 covers tuition or examination fees that qualify for the 8% tax credit rate. Line 40.6 is tuition or examination fees paid for 2025 if they exceed $100. Line 40.7 is the amount of tuition fees refunded under the Canada training credit from federal line 45350. Line 41 subtracts line 40.7 from line 40.6. Line 42 multiplies the amount from line 68 by 12.5. Line 43 subtracts line 42 from line 41. Line 44 is the unused portion from line 48 of Schedule T for 2024 or the notice of assessment. Line 44.1 adds lines 43 and 44. Line 45 multiplies line 44.1 by 8%. Line 46 is all or part of line 45 claimed on TP-1 line 398. Line 47 subtracts line 46 from line 45. Line 48 multiplies line 47 by 12.5 to determine the amount that can be carried forward.

Part B calculates the tax credit for tuition or examination fees transferred by a child. It applies only if the student transfers all or part of the tuition or examination fees entered on line 41; the student cannot transfer the part of the tax credit that relates to tuition or examination fees paid for previous years. Line 51 is the amount from line 41. Line 52 multiplies line 51 by 8%. Line 53 is the amount from TP-1 line 401. Line 54 is the total of TP-1 lines 378 and 381. Line 56 multiplies line 54 by 20%. Line 58 is the total of TP-1 lines 377.1, 390, 391, 392, 395, 396 and 397. Line 60 adds lines 56 and 58. Line 62 subtracts line 60 from line 53 and floors the result at zero. Line 66 subtracts line 62 from line 52 and floors the result at zero; this is the tax credit that can be transferred. Line 68 is all or part of line 66 transferred to the eligible person, who completes Part D of Schedule A and enters the transferred amount on line 65 of that schedule.
